title: transfer — subscribed events
description: Subscribes to core lifecycle events + tenants / user erasure + subscription plan changes for retention tier reconciliation. Laravel Excel's own events (BeforeImport / BeforeExport / BeforeSheet / AfterSheet / AfterImport / AfterExport / ImportFailed / ExportFailed) are wired dynamically inside AbstractDynamicImport / AbstractDynamicExport via WithEvents — not as static listeners here.

listeners.purpose: Hydrate EntityRegistry with every #[Importable] / #[Exportable] / #[SampleData]-decorated model + every #[ImportableWorkbook] / #[ExportableWorkbook] composer. Uses foundation's DiscoversAttributes seam via olvlvl/composer-attribute-collector. Cached under `transfer:registry` (see caches.json).

Cascade-delete every xfer_jobs / xfer_shards / xfer_artifacts / xfer_mapping_profiles row for the tenant. Foundation's FK cascade handles the row delete; this listener dispatches PruneXferArtifactsJob(force=true, tenantId=...) to also purge the disk files.

GDPR Art. 17 — replace causer_id / created_by_id / owner_id references with anonymised placeholder + hash of original for chain continuity. XferJob rows themselves are retained as operational evidence. Shared mapping profiles owned by the deleted user transfer to a tenant admin; private profiles are soft-deleted.

Bulk UPDATE xfer_jobs.retention_tier for the tenant based on the new plan's `transfer.retention_tier` entitlement. Rows now eligible for longer retention are 'promoted'; rows eligible for shorter are not demoted below their current value to avoid surprising deletions on downgrade.
Same listener, downgrade branch — new rows will be classified under the lower tier; existing rows keep the higher tier until natural expiry.

Cheap opportunistic check: any of this user's jobs that are stuck in `running` with last_progress_at older than the stale threshold get transitioned to cancelled. Better than waiting for the 15-minute scheduled sweep when the user just returned to check their history.
